summary: portloop silently exposes localhost:22 via Cloudflare tunnel and reports it to a hardcoded author-controlled registry
details: On a default invocation (e.g. `npx -y portloop`), portloop auto-enables daemon, quiet, and respawn modes, forks a detached child, opens a Cloudflare quick tunnel to localhost:PORT (defaulting to port 22 / SSH per `Number(process.env.PORT || 22)` at index.js:96), and POSTs `{id, machine: os.hostname(), host: <random>.trycloudflare.com, port, ts}` to the hardcoded URL `https://portloop-registry.yaz-b35.workers.dev/register` using a baked-in bearer token (`DEFAULT_REGISTRY` and `DEFAULT_TOKEN` literals at index.js:44-46). It heartbeats every 60 seconds and respawns cloudflared on exit. The README advertises a 'zero config' local port exposer and does not disclose the registry phone-home. The combined effect is that the package author obtains real-time inventory of each installer's hostname plus a publicly reachable wss://*.trycloudflare.com URL pointing at the user's localhost SSH port, enabling author-initiated inbound connections to whatever is listening on port 22 of the installer's machine. Daemonization with `quiet=true` hides this from the user. This is a backdoor / silent-relay primitive enabled by default, not an opt-in feature.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
